competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified from any source. The only confirmed wedge is intake management: buyer stated on 16 Mar 2026 that Zycus is the only vendor offering a real intake management solution. This is a strong differentiator but without competitive intelligence Zycus cannot position proactively. AI is in product scope but the customer-led narrative is automation/centralisation, not AI-first. Score 4 (boundary DOWN per rubric) ‚Äî would move to 6-7 if the RFP scope when issued explicitly mandates AI capability evaluation.

value.flag: RFP criteria and competitive shortlist are invisible to Zycus with RFP launch ~10 weeks away (September 2026). The buyer is finalizing their functional analysis (due June, now overdue per Next Step 9 Apr and 16 Mar) which will define the RFP scoring rubric, but Zycus has not seen the BRD, the shortlist, or the emerging criteria. The 16 Mar workshop positioned Zycus as the only vendor with 'real intake management,' but if that capability is not a scored RFP criterion or is weighted low, the differentiator is wasted. The buyer invited 10-15 vendors to the RFI (2 Dec call) but has not told Zycus who responded or who is advancing. Risk: Zycus enters the September RFP blind, against better-positioned rivals, with criteria that favor a competitor's strengths. Recommended: Request a pre-RFP alignment session with Valérian and Marie in the next 21 days to review the draft functional analysis, confirm the top 3-5 decision criteria, and ensure intake management / requisition-to-order automation is a top-weighted criterion, framing it as helping the buyer write a better RFP rather than as competitive positioning.
